scheme_name: Pension Scheme to Old Age Persons (Senior Citizen)
page_title: Pension Scheme to Old Age Persons (Senior Citizen)

details: The scheme “Pension Scheme to Old Age Persons (Senior Citizen)” is implemented by the Social Welfare Department, UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu. The scheme aims to support senior citizens in meeting their basic needs during old age. It is specifically targeted at residents of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu who are 60 years and above, with an annual family income not exceeding ₹1.5 lakh per annum. Under this scheme, eligible individuals aged 60–69 years receive a monthly pension of ₹1,500/-, while those aged 70 years and above receive ₹2,000/- per month.
benefits: 1. Senior citizens aged 60–69 years: Monthly pension of ₹1,500/-.
2. Senior citizens aged 70 years and above: Monthly pension of ₹2,000/-.
The mode of fund transfer shall be Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
eligibility: The applicant should be a domicile of the Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.
The applicant should be 60 years or older.
The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹1,50,000/-.
The applicant should not be receiving financial assistance under any other old age pension scheme.

offline: The applicant can collect the application form from the office of the District Panchayat or Municipal Council of the concerned district. Alternatively, the form can be
The applicant should carefully fill out the application form and attach all necessary documents.

The duly filled application form, addressed to the Chief Executive Officer/Chief Officer of the concerned District Panchayat/Municipal Council, should be submitted to the Gram Panchayat/District Panchayat/Municipal Council of the concerned district.

Field-level staff of the District Panchayat/Municipal Council will verify the submitted documents and scrutinize the application. They will also collect a life certificate from the beneficiaries annually.
The Chief Executive Officer/Chief Officer of the respective District Panchayat/Municipal Council will review and approve the applications.
• Application process: The application may be processed within 30 (thirty) days from the date of receipt.

• Payment disbursement: Once the application is approved by the Approving / Recommending authorities, the payment shall be disbursed into the bank account of the senior citizens by the fifth of every month.

For any grievance, the aggrieved senior citizen may approach the Chief Executive Officer/Chief Officer of District Panchayat / Municipal Council in the concerned district or District Collector of the concerned district or the Department of Social Welfare.

documents_required: Proof of Age (In case of non-availability of age proof the beneficiary shall be referred to the Medical Board (Government Hospital) of the concerned district for verification of age.)
Income Certificate issued by the Competent Authority
Domicile Certificate issued by the Competent Authority
Copy of Bank Pass Book (Aadhaar linked)
Undertaking/Affidavit (Not getting any pension from any other department) in the prescribed proforma at

faqs.question: What is the objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide financial support to senior citizens to help them meet their basic needs.

faqs.question: Who implements this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the Social Welfare Department, UT of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.

faqs.question: Is this a one-time benefit or a recurring pension?
faqs.answer: This is a monthly pension provided to eligible senior citizens.

faqs.question: What is the pension amount?
faqs.answer: The beneficiaries aged 60–69 years receive ₹1,500 per month, while those aged 70 years and above receive ₹2,000 per month.

faqs.question: How is the pension amount distributed?
faqs.answer: The pension is disbursed directly into the beneficiary's bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Any resident of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu who is 60 years or older and has an annual family income of ₹1.5 lakh or less is eligible.

faqs.question: Is there any domicile requirement for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must be a domicile of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu.

faqs.question: Can a senior citizen receiving another old-age pension apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, applicants should not be receiving financial assistance under any other old-age pension scheme.

faqs.question: Is there any requirement regarding financial status?
faqs.answer: Yes, the annual family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹1,50,000.

faqs.question: How can one apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants can collect the application form from the District Panchayat/Municipal Council office or download it from the official website.

faqs.question: Where should the completed application form be submitted?
faqs.answer: The duly filled form should be submitted to the Gram Panchayat/District Panchayat/Municipal Council of the concerned district.

faqs.question: How long does the application process take?
faqs.answer: The application is typically processed within 30 days from the date of submission.

faqs.question: When will the pension amount be credited?
faqs.answer: The pension is credited by the 5th of every month once the application is approved.

faqs.question: Can I receive the pension amount in cash?
faqs.answer: No, the pension amount is only transferred through a bank account.
